source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Observed reduced-form residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and diagonal idiosyncratic residual noise.
Noise is independent of structural shocks and idiosyncratic across residual equations, so V is diagonal and mixed noise cumulants vanish.
Structural shocks are normalized to unit variance; otherwise diagonal residual variance can be relabeled as shock variance.
Population sign-restricted set built from a covariance factor P_* P_*' = B0 B0' + V and restrictions R(P_* Q) >= 0.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: If V is nonzero, the standard sign-restricted population set is based on B0 B0' + V and generally contains neither B0 nor a positive column-rescaling B0 D.
B0(D^2 - I)B0' = V, equivalently D^2 - I = B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'}; the right side is generally not diagonal.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: Set of sign-admissible no-noise covariance factors whose recovered shocks B^{-1} u_t satisfy an independence criterion.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md

objects.description: Under independent non-Gaussian structural shocks and diagonal idiosyncratic residual noise, a no-noise K-shock demixing generally cannot produce mutually independent recovered shocks from the larger primitive source vector.

Drautzburg-Wright-like sign-restricted candidate set that searches over a normalized impact space and uses mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u, rather than the no-noise covariance factorization, under additive Gaussian residual noise.
objects.verification_status: M0010 working derivation complete; pending adversarial audit, simulation checks, and paper-structure decision
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md

objects.description: Moment-equation representation of the DW-like robust stack: third mixed central moments and fourth mixed cumulants of z(B)=B^{-1}u, with second moments entering only as nuisance covariance products inside fourth cumulants.
objects.verification_status: Pending independent algebra review and population verification

objects.description: Working local identification claim: in the bivariate normalized system, mixed higher cumulants of B(a,b)^{-1}u identify (a0,b0) locally under Gaussian residual noise when each structural shock has a nonzero third or fourth cumulant and 1-a0 b0 is nonzero.

Adversarial review of the Gaussian-noise DW-like higher-moment route, checking cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment nuisance treatment, normalization, local rank, and whether the route should replace or sit beside the BR-style observed-residual route.

Bivariate diagonal-normalized BR-style profiled cumulant set that profiles structural cumulants and diagonal-noise cumulants as needed, then intersects verified candidate matrices with sign labels; defined as distinct from direct quasi-JADE.
objects.verification_status: M07 applicability boundary complete; pending M08 adversarial review and M09 profiled criteria

objects.description: Complete bivariate cumulant system through fourth order with structural cumulants and diagonal noise cumulants profiled as nuisance parameters; audited as a working map, but still pending profiled-criterion, rank, and simulation verification.
objects.verification_status: M06 adversarial cumulant-map audit complete; pending M09 profiled criteria and M12 symbolic/population verification

objects.description: Expanded second-, third-, and fourth-order cumulant equations for B(a,b) = [[1,a],[b,1]], including nuisance noise cumulants nu_i, tau_i, and rho_i; M06 confirmed the coefficient pattern and corrected clean-equation versus identifying-restriction language.
objects.verification_status: M06 adversarial audit complete; pending M12 symbolic/population verification

objects.description: Applicability boundary showing that full Bonhomme-Robin quasi-JADE does not directly cover the bivariate L=K=2 SVAR because the clean-pair rank conditions fail; M08 still needs to attack this argument.
objects.verification_status: M07 boundary argument complete; pending M08 adversarial applicability review

objects.description: Minimal J_4(a,b) and stacked J_stack(a,b) profiled criteria; they must be kept distinct from mapped variance diagnostics and restricted no-noise J tests.
Bivariate fourth-cumulant rank condition a0 kappa_2 != b0 kappa_1 together with nonsingularity 1-a0 b0 != 0; must be rederived from the corrected cumulant map.

To-be-verified result: under diagonal noise, correct signs, compactness, valid nuisance profiling, and local rank, the bivariate BR-style profiled inversion targets the robust population set and contains B0.
Image of the robust accepted B(a,b) set under nu_1(a)=sigma_11-(1+a^2) and nu_2(b)=sigma_22-(1+b^2).

To-be-verified diagnostic result: if the profiled cumulant set is consistent and true diagonal noise is bounded away from zero, the mapped noise set excludes the no-noise origin asymptotically; under no noise it covers the origin at the intended rate.
Restricted J test that sets selected diagonal noise cumulants to zero and compares the no-noise SVAR fit with the unrestricted profiled cumulant fit.

Adversarial review of the BR-style derivation. M06 completed the cumulant-map portion, checking cumulant algebra, nuisance treatment, undefined objects, and overinterpretation; rank claims and boundary cases remain for M09/M10.
Adversarial review of BR-style verification simulations, checking DGP normalization, cumulant estimators, grids, critical values, seeds, failure cases, and interpretation.
Sign-boundary movement, response ellipse deformation, and column-rescaling obstruction under diagonal noise.
No-noise independence-refinement score under residual noise, including empty-set and high-noise reopening behavior.
BR robust accepted B region and image in implied diagonal-noise space.
Path of common diagonal noise and implied diagnostic behavior.

open_audits.question: Which planned objects should be promoted into main-text statements after Proposition 2 and the BR-style profiled cumulant results are independently derived, simulated, and adversarially reviewed?
Do analytic derivations and simulations actually verify the BR-style profiled cumulant inversion and mapped noise diagnostic, or should those claims be weakened or removed?

Does the Gaussian-noise DW-like higher-moment derivation survive adversarial audit strongly enough to simplify the paper structure, or should it remain a restricted benchmark beside the broader BR-style observed-residual route?
